Instructions for configuring Nginx as a reverse proxy for a WSGI server in a Flask application.

# Purpose
The document provides guidance on configuring Nginx as a reverse proxy for a WSGI server. It explains the role of Nginx in handling incoming HTTP requests, TLS, and other security and performance tasks more effectively than a WSGI server. The document outlines the basic steps to configure Nginx, including modifying the `nginx.conf` file to add a `server` section with a `proxy_pass` directive that points to the local address where the WSGI server listens. It also mentions the importance of setting appropriate headers to ensure the application correctly interprets forwarded requests. Additionally, it briefly touches on domain name configuration and how to simulate domain name resolution using the `hosts` file on Linux systems.
